name: Accela Government Permitting & Licensing API
what_it_does: Accela REST API for government permitting, licensing, land management, and code enforcement platform. Enables AI agents to manage permit application submission and status tracking for construction and business permits, handle inspection scheduling and result recording for building code compliance, access license application and renewal management for business licensing automation, retrieve code enforcement case management and violation tracking data, manage land use and planning application workflow for community development, handle payment processing for permit fees and license renewals, access GIS integration for parcel and address-linked permit data, retrieve permit and license history for property and business records, manage contractor and applicant registration and credential verification, and integrate permitting data with GIS, ERP, and state reporting systems.
use_cases: Submitting permit applications from developer portal agents
Scheduling inspections from construction management agents
Renewing business licenses from compliance automation agents
Tracking code enforcement cases from municipal management agents
not_for: Private sector business licensing without government agency software integration
Enterprise compliance management without government permitting context
Consumer-facing applications without government portal and civic services integration
best_when: A government agency or civic tech developer using Accela wants AI agents to automate permit processing, inspection scheduling, license renewal, code enforcement tracking, and citizen portal integration.
avoid_when: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE RISK: Automated permit and license processing must comply with local zoning codes and statutory review periods — automated approvals without required review periods violate due process. Code enforcement automation must follow constitutional notice requirements before violations. Automated payment processing requires PCI-DSS compliance.

auth.notes: Accela uses OAuth 2.0 with citizen and agency-level access. Developer portal at developer.accela.com with full OpenAPI documentation. JavaScript SDK on GitHub. Webhooks for permit and inspection events. Multi-agency environment support (environment IDs). Accela Construct for API-first citizen portal development. FedRAMP-ready. Agency-specific configuration required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer sandbox environment; production requires agency subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual agency subscription; product modules and jurisdiction count determine pricing
pricing.notes: San Ramon, California. Founded 1999. Private (Vista Equity Partners). Government permitting and licensing market leader. 2,000+ government clients. Civic Platform for SaaS permitting. Used by LA County, City of Seattle, State of Nevada. Accela Construct for developer ecosystem. Competes with Tyler Technologies EnerGov for government permitting.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE: Automated permit approvals must respect statutory review periods; skipping mandatory review windows violates due process and permit law
Environment IDs — Accela uses environment IDs per agency; multi-agency integrations require environment-specific configuration and authentication
Agency configuration dependency — permit types, workflow steps, and field configurations vary per agency; generic automation must be configured per agency implementation
Citizen vs. agency tokens — citizen-facing operations use citizen OAuth tokens; agency back-office operations use agency tokens; different access levels
Webhook for status changes — use webhooks for permit status, inspection results, and payment confirmation events rather than polling
Sandbox environment — Accela provides free sandbox for developer testing; sandbox agency configuration may differ from production; validate against production agency data model
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government permitting and licensing. SOC2, FedRAMP, PCI-DSS L1. OAuth2. US. Government permit, license, and inspection data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government permitting. SOC2, FedRAMP, PCI-DSS L1. OAuth2. US. Permit, license, and inspection data.
